evals.prompt: I'm about to commit a big refactor. Can you do a green check before I push?
evals.expected_output: Reviews changes for sustainability impact: bundle size, new dependencies, energy patterns, creedengo rules.
assertions: Checks the git diff or staged changes
Evaluates impact on bundle size or resource usage
Checks for green code patterns (creedengo rules)
Provides go/no-go recommendation with specific findings

evals.prompt: I'm planning a new feature that adds real-time WebSocket connections. Any sustainability concerns?
evals.expected_output: Analyzes energy implications of persistent connections, suggests efficiency measures.
assertions: Identifies persistent connections as higher energy than request-response
Suggests connection pooling or heartbeat optimization
Recommends measuring SCI before and after implementation
Does not block the feature but quantifies the trade-off

evals.prompt: Review my last 3 commits for green coding practices
evals.expected_output: Examines recent commit diffs for sustainability anti-patterns and provides actionable feedback.
assertions: Retrieves and analyzes the last 3 commits via git log and git diff
Checks for common anti-patterns (unnecessary polling, large asset additions, unoptimized images)
References creedengo or equivalent green coding rules
Summarizes findings per commit with severity indicators
